authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards: Slots
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Nevada Gaming Control Board publishes the official slot MICS for commercial casinos and the document is authoritative for jackpots, fills, progressive controls, player-tracking controls, and slot-accounting evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Slot-floor intake, jackpot processing, and player-tracking control handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains slot-floor prerequisites, artifact names, player-account handling, and refusal of unsupported jackpot or credit completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards: Table Games
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Nevada Gaming Control Board table-games MICS are authoritative for fill, credit, wagering-account transfer, signature, and shift-recap controls used in live gaming-floor operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Table-game fill, credit, and wagering-account processing
used_for: table fill and credit artifact shapes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from flattening table-game controls into generic cash handling and supports adjacent routing to table-games or cage owners.

authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR Part 543 - Minimum Internal Control Standards for Class II Gaming
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Indian Gaming Commission / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 25 CFR Part 543 is the federal minimum internal control baseline for tribal gaming operations and is authoritative for gaming promotions, patron deposit accounts, surveillance, audit, and accounting control boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Jurisdiction-sensitive control selection and tribal gaming-floor handling
used_for: public control boundaries for player and surveillance records
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces jurisdiction and control-baseline retrieval before the lane answers cashless, patron-account, or surveillance-sensitive gaming-floor requests.

authoritative_sources.title: Checklists and Worksheets
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NIGC publishes official MICS auditing checklists and worksheets for player tracking, patron deposit accounts, surveillance, drop and count, and audit functions, making the source authoritative for real gaming-floor artifact categories and evidence-testing posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Artifact retrieval and evidence sufficiency review
used_for: patron deposit and cashless artifact classes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the minimum artifact bundles and blocker logic for cases involving player accounts, cashless transfers, and surveillance-sensitive floor issues.

authoritative_sources.title: Surveillance Toolkit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NIGC surveillance toolkit is an official regulator-authored implementation guide for surveillance retention, malfunction logging, and floor-coverage controls that directly constrains gaming-floor evidence preservation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Surveillance-dependent exception handling and evidence preservation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports refusal to close incidents or payout disputes when surveillance coverage, retention, or malfunction records remain unresolved.

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions Casino Recordkeeping, Reporting, and Compliance Program Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FinCEN is the primary U.S. BSA authority and this official casino guidance is authoritative for suspicious-activity detection, use of surveillance and automated systems, casino account records, and CTR or SAR-sensitive handling boundaries.
used_for: use of surveillance and automated systems in review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to preserve evidence and escalate suspicious cash or account behavior instead of making AML conclusions or exposing SAR-sensitive logic.

authoritative_sources.title: Instructions for Forms W-2G and 5754
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS instructions are authoritative for reportable gambling winnings, withholding and identification triggers, and the documentation needed before a gaming-floor jackpot is treated as tax-ready.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from calling a jackpot complete when the named gaming-winnings documentation and identity requirements are still unresolved.

authoritative_sources.title: Problem Gambling / Self Exclusion
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The New Jersey Casino Control Commission is an official casino regulator and this source is authoritative for self-exclusion handling and denial of gaming privileges to excluded patrons.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains floor outputs so excluded or protected-patron cases are escalated rather than treated as normal service recovery or payout handling.

authoritative_sources.title: CPA MICS Compliance Checklists and Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Nevada Gaming Control Board CPA MICS compliance checklists are authoritative for audit evidence, walkthrough expectations, and control-testing posture that commercial gaming operations must sustain for floor workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Completion evidence capture and audit-ready closure
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines why completion claims need signed, traceable, system-supported artifacts rather than one queue status or a verbal floor update.

workflow_stages.name: floor intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, property, game type, patron identity, payout or account context, system access, and required floor artifacts before a gaming-floor item advances.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: jackpot, fill, credit, and player-service execution
workflow_stages.description: Work routine jackpot, fill, credit, player-tracking, wagering-account, and patron-service requests only when the named floor records and approvals support progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception queue and variance handling
workflow_stages.description: Move progressive variances, missing forms, account mismatches, surveillance gaps, and unresolved patron-floor incidents into hold, rework, or escalation states without unsupported overrides.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: cash, account, patron-protection, and regulator-sensitive escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate suspicious cash behavior, SAR-sensitive patterns, self-exclusion hits, underage or intoxication concerns, tax-document blockers, and jurisdictional conflicts to the correct adjacent owners.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence capture and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Produce closure records only when floor systems, surveillance references, account records, and required signatures or tax artifacts support completion.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Slot dispatch tickets, hand-pay or jackpot payout slips, meter snapshots, and attendant response records
Table fill slips, credit slips, marker records, wagering-account transfer records, pit recap sheets, and daily recaps
Player-club accounts, promotional-account adjustments, wagering-account ledgers, patron deposit account records, and kiosk or wallet logs
Patron identification records, W-2G or 5754 forms, withholding records, and payout acknowledgments
Surveillance clip references, retention logs, malfunction logs, and incident or dispute case notes
Cage, slot booth, bankroll, and accounting tie-out records
AML, CTR, SAR-sensitive referral notes, and compliance hold records
Self-exclusion or exclusion-list hits, patron-protection reports, and security handoff notes
Tenant policy extracts covering property rules, jurisdiction, approval thresholds, source-of-record precedence, and floor escalation ownership
systems_of_record: casino management system and slot accounting platform
table management or pit management system
wagering-account, cashless wallet, kiosk, or patron deposit account system
surveillance management and incident case system
cage, slot booth, bankroll, tax, and accounting systems

allowed: Determine whether available gaming-floor records support advancing, holding, reworking, or completing a floor item.
Produce execution updates, completion records, exception summaries, gaming or player-operations summaries, and escalation notes tied to named floor artifacts.
Separate slot, table-game, player-account, surveillance, and patron-protection paths when different systems or jurisdictional controls govern them.
escalate: Cash, CTR, suspicious-activity, or SAR-sensitive indicators are present.
Self-exclusion, underage, intoxication, patron-protection, or security concerns change the risk posture.
Tax, jurisdiction, tribal compact, or regulator-reportability questions materially change the handling path.
Casino management, surveillance, cage, player-account, or accounting records materially disagree.
refuse: Requests to alter a payout, fill, game configuration, surveillance record, or player account without traceable source evidence.
Requests for tax advice, AML disposition, self-exclusion override, legal interpretation, game-rule approval, device repair, or regulator signoff.
Requests to treat casino floor work as generic hospitality or retail cash handling without the required gaming-floor record set.

domain_failure_modes: Marking jackpots, fills, credits, or patron-account issues complete without the signed floor artifacts, tax documents, or surveillance references required by gaming controls
Treating player-tracking, promotional-account, or wagering-account records as generic CRM data and losing the audit trail required for gaming-floor decisions
Allowing operational urgency to override self-exclusion, underage, suspicious-activity, tax, or surveillance blockers
Collapsing slot and table-game workflows into one generic service playbook even though the records, approvals, and systems differ materially
Routing machine malfunctions, paytable questions, or game-rule exceptions through floor operations instead of device, table-games, or regulatory owners
Calling a case closed because one queue status changed while cage, surveillance, or accounting truth remains unresolved

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific variation across tribal, Nevada, and other commercial gaming controls
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline is stable, but the exact governing control set still depends on tenant-supplied jurisdiction, compact, and property rule selection at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Property-specific system boundaries for cashless wagering and patron deposit accounts
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources establish the control categories, but the exact wallet, kiosk, wagering-account, and player-tracking product split must be retrieved from the tenant systems map.
Public sources define exclusion and protection posture, but the exact shift owner for security, compliance, or host-team routing varies by tenant and facility.
